title: JavaScript Array.prototype.sort() sorts elements as strings by default
rule: Array.prototype.sort() with no comparator converts each element to a string and sorts lexicographically, so [10, 2, 1].sort() yields [1, 10, 2], not [1, 2, 10]. Pass a numeric comparator: arr.sort((a, b) => a - b).
triggers: JavaScript Array.sort returns numbers in the wrong order
Array.sort is comparing numbers as strings
